This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its unique flower and leaf morphology. The genus Oenothera is further divided into several subgenera, with Oenothera mckelveyae belonging to the subgenus Campestries. The plant's classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary history and ecological role in its native habitat.

intro: Oenothera mckelveyae, also known as McKelvey's evening primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. The health benefits of Oenothera mckelveyae include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid. The bioactive constituents of Oenothera mckelveyae include GLA, as well as other polyphenolic compounds and flavonoids that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as oils, capsules, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote cardiovascular well-being.
